prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh sweetheart, the CPU is sort of like the brain of the computer—the part that does the thinking and tells the other parts what to do. When you click on something, the CPU races through lots of tiny instructions, like a very fast cook following a recipe step by step. I don’t know all the fancy details, but I do know it works with the memory to keep track of what it’s doing for the moment. A faster CPU makes the computer feel quicker, like having a speedy reader leading the class. It doesn’t store your pictures forever; it just does the quick thinking so everything else can happen in the right order.
